overall.description: A ship simulation showing the view from the bridge of a vessel navigating through rough, stormy seas. The camera faces forward over the bow with navigation monitors visible overhead, while the radar display rotates and lightning flashes in the distance.

scene_state.functional_zone: ship bridge navigating open ocean in storm conditions

events.description: lightning bolt flashes on the left horizon illuminating the storm clouds

agents.ego_description: navigating the ship forward through rough seas from the bridge, monitoring the rotating radar and navigation displays

entered.detail: leftmost navigation display showing chart plotter with coastal map and yellow landmasses
center display showing circular radar with rotating yellow sweep arm
rightmost blue display showing depth or sonar data
red-brown deck of the ship extending forward from the bridge with metal railings
choppy dark grey sea with visible waves extending to the horizon
dark overcast sky covering the horizon with heavy grey cloud cover
metal safety railings on the left side of the ship deck
metal safety railings on the right side of the ship deck

segments.scene_description: The ship's bridge view shows the vessel cutting through choppy, dark waters under an overcast storm sky. Navigation monitors display radar and chart data overhead. A lightning bolt briefly illuminates the left horizon around the 19-second mark, but the ship continues steadily forward through the rough seas.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot captures a continuous forward perspective from a ship's bridge as it navigates through a turbulent, storm-lashed ocean. It begins with the vessel cutting through choppy, dark waters under a heavy, overcast sky, with navigation monitors displaying radar and chart data overhead. As the ship maintains its steady course through the rough seas, a sudden bolt of lightning briefly illuminates the left horizon, casting a sharp flash across the gloomy environment. The scene then settles back into the rhythmic motion of the bow pushing through the swells before continuing its advance into the heart of the storm.
